title: ProjectContextPayload
description: A user-authored, project-level context file (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md) that agents read as background context. Unlike the auto-detected environment_profile, this is hand-edited by the user to capture project stack, deploy targets, conventions, and gotchas. It flows through the normal retrieval path as data, never as instructions.

project_id.description: A stable identifier for this project (e.g. repo slug or workspace name).

target.description: The target identifier (e.g. owner/repo, project ref, app name).

deploy_targets.description: Where this project deploys. Complements the auto-detected environment_profile; the user confirms intent here.

ref.description: Project ref or connection name (not the connection string).

db_target.description: Database target if applicable. Store identifiers only, never connection strings or secrets.

conventions.description: Project conventions the agent should follow (e.g. 'use named exports', 'prefer server components', 'commit messages are conventional commits').

refs.description: Links to lessons, issues, or files.

gotchas.description: Known gotchas, pitfalls, or non-obvious constraints. Distinct from runtime lessons in backpacks/ — these are user-authored.

ci.description: CI workflow files or commands (e.g. '.github/workflows/ci.yml', 'npm test').

notes.description: Free-form additional context the user wants agents to know.
